data_layer_convention.name: Default web dataLayer
data_layer_convention.notes: Clear event_data or ecommerce before replacing a previously populated object.

journeys.name: Quote request
journeys.business_goal: Measure intentional quote starts and accepted leads.

events.definition: Provides the reusable page and user context needed by subsequent measurement.
events.trigger: Push on every full page load and SPA route change after the new page state is available.

parameters.name: page_template
parameters.definition: Identifies the stable functional template of the displayed page or route.
parameters.value_rule: Use the normalized functional page template.

parameters.name: login_status
parameters.definition: Indicates whether the current visitor is authenticated.
parameters.value_rule: Use one of the exhaustive normalized states.

custom_decision.business_need: Compare journeys for authenticated and unauthenticated visitors.
custom_decision.official_candidate: No prescribed event parameter represents reusable authentication state.
custom_decision.why_not_fit: The value is persistent user context rather than the outcome of the official login event.

events.definition: This event signifies that some content was shown to the user. Use this event to discover the most popular items viewed.
events.trigger: Push once after the primary product detail and selected default variant are rendered.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.definition: Currency of the items associated with the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * Value metrics on the view_item event to not contribute to revenue * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.definition: The monetary value of the event. * Set value to the sum of (price * quantity) for all items in items. Don't include shipping or tax. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.
parameters.value_rule: Use the displayed item price for the selected variant.

parameters.name: items
parameters.value_rule: Send one item object for the displayed product variant.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.definition: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.
parameters.value_rule: Use the stable SKU or product identifier.

parameters.name: item_name
parameters.definition: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.
parameters.value_rule: Use the stable catalog product name.

parameters.name: item_variant
parameters.definition: The item variant or unique code or description for additional item details/options.
parameters.value_rule: Use the primary normalized size or variant representation.

parameters.name: item_color
parameters.definition: Identifies the normalized color selected for the item.
parameters.value_rule: Use the normalized catalog color label.

custom_decision.business_need: Analyse color preference independently from size.
custom_decision.why_not_fit: item_variant represents the primary size or variant; it cannot isolate color when both dimensions are selected.

events.definition: Indicates that a visitor intentionally started the quote-request journey.
events.trigger: Push once when the visitor enters the first interactive quote step after selecting a quote call to action.

parameters.name: form_name
parameters.definition: Identifies the form whose journey was started.
parameters.value_rule: Use the stable functional form name.

custom_decision.business_need: Compare quote starts for reusable form implementations.
custom_decision.official_candidate: No prescribed begin_quote parameter identifies the form.
custom_decision.why_not_fit: A stable form identifier is required to analyse multiple quote entry points.

parameters.name: project_type
parameters.condition: Send when the project type is known at journey start.
parameters.definition: Identifies the normalized project selected for the quote request.
parameters.value_rule: Use one of the exhaustive website project types.

custom_decision.business_need: Analyse quote starts by requested project.
custom_decision.official_candidate: No official begin_quote parameter represents the requested project.
custom_decision.why_not_fit: The project is a business-specific quote dimension.

custom_decision.business_need: Measure intentional entry into a quote journey before a lead exists.
custom_decision.why_not_fit: The business requires a quote-specific funnel start across interfaces that do not all expose a standard form start.
